title: Theory-Forge Tool Development Timeline
description: Automation built in response to paper development challenges

events.triggered_by_problem: Need structured workflow for data-to-paper pipeline
events.detail: Initial release with 8 core agents covering the full pipeline from data exploration to manuscript generation. Basic workflow without state tracking or feedback loops.

events.triggered_by_problem: Need to document external verification step
events.detail: Updated tutorial to include Phase 9 (external verification) and separate data sources for each paper.

events.triggered_by_problem: Papers required 5-8 complete reframings each. Lost track of frame iterations across 4 simultaneous papers. Needed way to save empirical work while pivoting theory.
events.detail: Added centralized state tracking via state.json. Tracks workflow progress, frame iterations, and metadata. Supports multi-project workspaces for working on 4 papers simultaneously.

events.triggered_by_problem: Invested weeks in theory development for patterns that violated no established prediction—just literature gaps. Wasted effort on non-puzzles.
events.detail: Full 10-criteria evaluation based on Ezra Zuckerman's 'Tips for Article-Writers'. Checks: Is this a real-world puzzle? Is the null compelling? Clear audience? This prevents the common 'literature gap' trap.

events.triggered_by_problem: Full Zuckerman evaluation too heavy for early-stage work. Needed quick gate AFTER pattern discovery but BEFORE theory investment.
events.detail: Restructured workflow to integrate Zuckerman checks earlier. Added /eval-zuckerman-lite as 3-criteria early gate: (1) Puzzle in the world? (2) Compelling null? (3) Enough 'there there'? Runs after /hunt-patterns, before /find-theory.

events.triggered_by_problem: Claude defaulted to wrong genre: bullet points (violates journal style), passive voice (econ/strategy register), fabricated statistics, orphaned empirical claims. Systematic gap analysis comparing published papers (Shadow Learning, Inverted Apprenticeships) to Claude-drafted papers.
events.detail: Full validation system for ASQ/OrgSci/ManSci papers. Hard rules: no bullets, no numbered lists, narrative contributions. Soft rules: <30% passive voice, all statistics interpreted, quotes as rare gems. Data hallucination prevention via inventory scanning.

events.triggered_by_problem: Papers use qualitative data under IRB protection—raw transcripts can't be shared with reviewers. But without verifiable evidence, claims look unsupported. Needed verification mechanism that works within privacy constraints.
events.detail: Bidirectional claim-evidence traceability system. Components: CLI for init/ingest/lint/export, PII hashing, evidence access tiers (PUBLIC/CONTROLLED/WITNESS_ONLY), k-anonymity for redaction. Pre-review workflow with quant/qual hierarchy adjudication.

events.triggered_by_problem: Non-technical reviewers need to inspect verification without using CLI. Needed standalone HTML exports.
events.detail: Flask web interface for reviewers. Static HTML exports for non-technical reviewers. PII entity redaction. Prevalence metadata (informant_coverage, contradicting_count, saturation_note). Badge visualization for contradicting evidence.

events.triggered_by_problem: Manual steps between /audit-claims and Living Paper ingestion. Needed seamless bridge.
events.detail: Automated /verify-claims to call Living Paper commands. Bridge between /audit-claims output and Living Paper ingestion. Seamless reviewer package generation.

events.triggered_by_problem: Single-run LLM analysis is non-reproducible. Different prompts → different answers. Peer reviewers will ask: 'How confident are you? Could you have gotten different results?'
events.detail: N-run execution with statistical aggregation. Default runs: /hunt-patterns n=25, /mine-qual n=15, /verify-claims n=10. Stability ratings: HIGH (CV<10%), MEDIUM (CV 10-25%), LOW (CV>25%). Quote stability tracking to catch cherry-picking.

events.triggered_by_problem: Manual quality assessment is inconsistent. Needed systematic scoring to prevent cherry-picking best version.
events.detail: Rubric-based scoring for paper quality, claim verification, and Zuckerman criteria. Updated /verify-claims and /eval-zuckerman to use rubric-based scoring. Made rubric-eval default with graceful fallback.

events.triggered_by_problem: When sending papers to external AI for adversarial review, needed to instruct HOW to review at journal submission standards. Can't assume reviewers know what 'good feedback' means.
events.detail: Template for external reviewers with systematic point-by-point feedback structure: claim-by-claim assessment, evidence audit, clarity checks, theory stress test. Integrated with /package-verification.

events.triggered_by_problem: Generic 'good paper' rubrics failed qual-inductive papers (Papers 1, 3, 4). Inductive papers have different structures: theory develops THROUGH findings, quotes do heavy lifting, progressive development replaces hypothesis testing.
events.detail: Genre-specific validation rules for qual-forward vs quant-forward papers. Different section requirements, quote allowances. 9 exemplar papers documented including 2 multimethod exemplars.

events.triggered_by_problem: When rewriting Paper 1 to inductive structure, LLM accidentally wrote in confirmatory style ('We expected to find X, and we did') rather than inductive style ('The data revealed X'). Needed semantic coherence check.
events.detail: LLM-based semantic analysis detecting theory section pre-announcement. Catches hypo-deductive framing in inductive papers. New violation types: EXPECTED_PATTERNS_SECTION, PATTERN_NUMBERING, ENUMERATED_CONTRIBUTIONS, SPECULATIVE_FINDINGS. Tested against 9 exemplar papers.

events.triggered_by_problem: Name 'paper-mining-agent-suite' was unwieldy. Needed memorable brand.
events.detail: Renamed from paper-mining-agent-suite to theory-forge. Updated README and all documentation.

events.triggered_by_problem: Needed end-to-end pipeline orchestration and additional evaluation dimensions beyond Zuckerman and genre.
events.detail: Added pipeline orchestration, Becker generalization test, enhanced genre checking with argument construction, and phenomenon-naming command. Additional evals (eval-limitations, eval-citations, eval-contribution) followed in subsequent commits.

events.triggered_by_problem: Theory-forge initially built for Matt's workflow. Students and collaborators need guided mode with prerequisites, advisor oversight, and gentler onramps.
events.detail: Added student mode integration, adversarial evidence as standard practice, prerequisite documentation, and advisor guide. Extensibility system: registry for custom data sources, methodology authoring.

events.triggered_by_problem: Needed systematic measurement capabilities (GABRIEL) and empirically-validated quality rules.
events.detail: Added /measure-at-scale for GABRIEL integration. Title grammar module added separately in commit 8d2de3e (Feb 28). Added register metrics, journal-specific exemplars, and paragraph granularity checking.

events.triggered_by_problem: After running theory-forge evaluations and generating revisions, users need to review AI-created changes with confidence. Built annotated HTML diff viewer comparing original vs proposed manuscript.
events.detail: Built /review-diff command and standalone diff-review.py tool. Generates annotated HTML diff viewer with per-hunk rationale banners, GitHub-style dark theme, collapsible hunks, keyboard shortcuts. Handles Overleaf download naming conventions. Zero external dependencies.

tool_problem_mapping.state.json: Lost track of frame iterations across 4 papers
tool_problem_mapping.style_enforcer: Claude defaulted to wrong genre (bullets, passive voice, hallucinated stats)
tool_problem_mapping.living_paper: IRB-protected data can't be shared; claims look unsupported
tool_problem_mapping.coherence_validator: LLM accidentally wrote confirmatory-style in inductive papers
tool_problem_mapping.eval-becker: No systematic test for theoretical generalizability
tool_problem_mapping.student_mode: Students need guided onramp, not expert pipeline
tool_problem_mapping.diff-review: Users need to review AI-created manuscript changes with confidence
